summary: Introduction to word processing concepts, working with LibreOffice Writer, document creation and management, text editing and formatting, table manipulation, mail merge, and shortcut keys.

topics.notes: Steps to launch a word processing application such as LibreOffice Writer, including accessing it through the Start menu, desktop shortcut, or application launcher.

topics.topic: Title Bar, Menu Bar, Toolbars and Sidebar
topics.notes: Key interface components of a word processor: the Title Bar displays the document name, the Menu Bar provides access to all commands, Toolbars offer quick-access buttons, and the Sidebar provides additional formatting and navigation options.
Methods to create a new blank document or use templates in a word processing application, including using the File menu or keyboard shortcut Ctrl+N.
Opening existing documents from local storage or recent files list using the File menu or the Ctrl+O keyboard shortcut.
Saving a document using Save (Ctrl+S) to overwrite the existing file, or Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S) to save with a new name, location, or file format.
Properly closing an open document using the File menu or Ctrl+W, with a prompt to save unsaved changes before closing.
Accessing the built-in Help feature (F1) in a word processor to find guidance on commands, features, and troubleshooting.
Configuring document layout settings such as paper size, orientation (portrait or landscape), margins, and page borders through the Page Setup dialog.
Viewing a document as it will appear when printed, allowing review of layout, margins, and formatting before printing.
Sending a document to a printer, with options to select the printer, number of copies, page range, and print quality.

topics.topic: PDF file and Saving a Document as PDF file
topics.notes: Understanding the PDF format and exporting or saving a word processing document as a PDF file using the Export as PDF option for universal readability.
Typing and entering content in a word processing document, including text, numbers, symbols, and special characters.
Modifying existing content in a document by inserting, deleting, overwriting, and replacing text.
Techniques to select text using the mouse or keyboard, including selecting a word, line, paragraph, or the entire document using Ctrl+A.
Moving (Cut: Ctrl+X) or duplicating (Copy: Ctrl+C) selected text and placing it at a new location (Paste: Ctrl+V) within the same or different document.

topics.topic: Font, Color, Style and Size Selection
topics.notes: Changing the appearance of text by selecting font type, font size, font color, and applying styles such as Bold (Ctrl+B), Italic (Ctrl+I), and Underline (Ctrl+U).
Positioning text horizontally on a page: Left (Ctrl+L), Center (Ctrl+E), Right (Ctrl+R), or Justify (Ctrl+J) alignment.
Reversing the last action using Undo (Ctrl+Z) and reapplying it using Redo (Ctrl+Y) to manage editing mistakes efficiently.
Automatic correction of common typing errors using AutoCorrect, and checking documents for spelling and grammar mistakes using the built-in checker (F7).
Locating specific text in a document using Find (Ctrl+F) and substituting it with different text using Replace (Ctrl+H).
Adjusting the left and right indentation of paragraphs to structure document content, using the ruler or paragraph formatting dialog.
Applying bullet points or numbered lists to items for organized presentation of information in a document.
Converting selected text between uppercase, lowercase, title case, and sentence case using the Change Case formatting option.
Adding repeating content at the top (Header) and bottom (Footer) of each page, such as page numbers, document title, date, or author name.
Inserting a table with a specified number of rows and columns, or drawing a custom table, to organize data within a document.
Resizing table rows and columns by dragging cell borders or using the table properties dialog to set precise dimensions.
Setting the horizontal and vertical alignment of content within table cells for proper presentation of tabular data.

topics.topic: Delete and Insertion of Row, Column and Merging and Splitting of Cells
topics.notes: Modifying table structure by adding or removing rows and columns, and combining multiple cells (Merge) or dividing a cell into smaller cells (Split).
Applying borders of different styles and colors to table cells or paragraphs, and adding background shading to enhance visual presentation.
A feature that automates the creation of personalized documents (letters, labels, envelopes) by combining a main document template with a data source containing recipient information.

Commonly used keyboard shortcuts in word processing: Ctrl+N (New), Ctrl+O (Open), Ctrl+S (Save), Ctrl+P (Print), Ctrl+Z (Undo), Ctrl+Y (Redo), Ctrl+X (Cut), Ctrl+C (Copy), Ctrl+V (Paste), Ctrl+A (Select All), Ctrl+F (Find), Ctrl+H (Replace), Ctrl+B (Bold), Ctrl+I (Italic), Ctrl+U (Underline), F7 (Spell Check).
